title: The Mapmaker's Dream
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A cartographer for the East India Company maps a river through Central Africa that leads to a city that appears on no other map.
long_description: London, 1874. Arthur Finch is a meticulous cartographer for the British East India Company who is sent to map the uncharted interior of Central Africa. Local rumors speak of a great river that leads to a city of stone, a place that appears on no European map. With his native guide Kibo, Arthur traces the river through jungle, rapids, and highlands. At its end he finds a walled city — ancient, inhabited, and deliberately omitted from every chart. The city's Guardian tests Arthur's intentions: will he draw the truth and destroy the city's protection, or leave it unwritten and betray his life's calling?

characters.Arthur Finch: A London cartographer, precise and methodical. Believes the world can be known through measurement.
characters.Kibo: A guide from a coastal trading village. Speaks three languages, reads no maps. He knows the river by memory and the city by legend.
characters.The Guardian: An elder woman of the stone city. She keeps the archive and determines who may know of the city's existence.
